Malay version of SAQLI
Daily functioning3.9 (0.87)2.1-5.70.00.0

page.content: In Table 1, which presents data on sex, prevalence, and parasitological results, the Male group consists of 21 individuals, showing a prevalence of 8 (38.1%) for parasitological tests and 17 (81.0%) for PCR tests. The Female group includes 34 individuals, revealing a prevalence of 16 (47.1%) for parasitological tests and 24 (70.6%) for PCR tests. The Total category aggregates 55 individuals, with a prevalence of 24 (43.6%) for parasitological tests and 41 (74.5%) for PCR tests. The p-value for the parasitological data is 0.515, and for PCR data, it is 0.391. In Table 2, which details the Malay version of SAQLI across various domains, the Daily functioning mean is reported as 3.9 with a standard deviation of 0.87, a range of 2.1-5.7, and both % Floor and % Ceiling at 0.0. Social interactions have a mean of 4.9 and a standard deviation of 0.95, with a range of 2.7-6.1, alongside % Floor and % Ceiling both at 0.0. Emotional functioning shows a mean of 4.4, a standard deviation of 0.84, a range of 2.2-5.6, and % Floor and % Ceiling at 0.0. The Total scale reports a mean of 4.4, a standard deviation of 0.83, a range of 2.4-5.7, with % Floor and % Ceiling both at 0.0.
